---
title: "Anti-Piracy System"
subtitle: "License Validation with Heartbeat Mechanism"
author: "Gustavo Reis"
institute: "GETGAIN Solutions"
format:
  revealjs:
    theme: [default, custom.scss]
    slide-number: true
    chalkboard: true
    logo: logo-getgain.png
    footer: "GProd Licensing System"
    transition: slide
    background-transition: fade
    highlight-style: github
    code-line-numbers: false
    width: 1920
    height: 1080
---

## Overview

Software piracy protection system combining:

- Hardware fingerprinting
- License server validation
- Periodic heartbeat mechanism
- Cryptographic security

::: {.notes}
This presentation covers the complete implementation of an anti-piracy system for GProd, our Blazor-based product.
:::

---

## Architecture
```{mermaid}
flowchart TB
    Client[GProd Client]
    Server[License Server]
    DB[(License Database)]
    
    Client -->|Heartbeat Request| Server
    Server -->|Validate| DB
    Server -->|Response| Client
    Client -->|Store Encrypted| Local[Local Storage]
    
    style Client fill:#2c5282
    style Server fill:#2d3748
    style DB fill:#1a365d
```

---

## Key Components

**Server Side:**

- License validation API
- Hardware fingerprint tracking
- Device limit enforcement
- Revocation management

**Client Side:**

- Background heartbeat service
- Local cache with grace period
- Hardware fingerprint generation
- Tamper detection

---

## Security Layers

1. **Request signing** - HMAC validation
2. **Response signing** - Prevent tampering
3. **Hardware binding** - Device fingerprinting
4. **Encrypted storage** - AES-256
5. **Grace period** - Tamper-resistant timestamps

---

# Server Implementation

---

## Heartbeat Endpoint
```csharp
[HttpPost("api/license/heartbeat")]
public async Task<IActionResult> Heartbeat(
    [FromBody] HeartbeatRequest request)
{
    // 1. Validate signature
    if (!ValidateRequestSignature(request))
        return Unauthorized();

    // 2. Check license status
    var license = await _db.Licenses
        .FirstOrDefaultAsync(l => l.Key == request.LicenseKey);
    
    if (license == null || license.IsRevoked)
        return Ok(new { Valid = false });
    
    // ... continued
}
```

---

## Device Registration
```csharp
// Verify hardware fingerprint
var deviceHash = HashHardwareFingerprint(request.HardwareInfo);

var device = license.RegisteredDevices
    .FirstOrDefault(d => d.FingerprintHash == deviceHash);

if (device == null)
{
    // New device - check limit
    if (license.RegisteredDevices.Count >= license.MaxDevices)
        return Ok(new { 
            Valid = false, 
            Reason = "Device limit exceeded" 
        });
    
    // Register new device
    license.RegisteredDevices.Add(new RegisteredDevice {
        FingerprintHash = deviceHash,
        FirstSeen = DateTime.UtcNow
    });
}
```

---

## Response Generation
```csharp
// Generate signed response with jitter
var response = new HeartbeatResponse
{
    Valid = true,
    NextCheckIn = DateTime.UtcNow
        .AddHours(24 + Random.Shared.Next(-2, 2)),
    ServerTimestamp = DateTime.UtcNow
};

// Sign the response
response.Signature = SignResponse(response, license.Key);

return Ok(response);
```

::: {.notes}
Random jitter makes the pattern unpredictable to attackers
:::

---

## Data Models
```csharp
public class HeartbeatRequest
{
    public string LicenseKey { get; set; }
    public string HardwareInfo { get; set; }
    public DateTime ClientTimestamp { get; set; }
    public string Signature { get; set; }
}

public class HeartbeatResponse
{
    public bool Valid { get; set; }
    public string Reason { get; set; }
    public DateTime NextCheckIn { get; set; }
    public DateTime ServerTimestamp { get; set; }
    public string Signature { get; set; }
}
```

---

# Client Implementation

---

## Background Service
```csharp
public class LicenseHeartbeatService : BackgroundService
{
    private const int GracePeriodDays = 7;
    
    protected override async Task ExecuteAsync(
        CancellationToken stoppingToken)
    {
        while (!stoppingToken.IsCancellationRequested)
        {
            try
            {
                var result = await PerformHeartbeat();
                
                if (!result.Valid)
                    await HandleInvalidLicense(result.Reason);
                else
                    SaveLastSuccessfulHeartbeat(result.ServerTimestamp);
                
                await Task.Delay(CalculateDelay(result), stoppingToken);
            }
            catch
            {
                await HandleNetworkFailure();
            }
        }
    }
}
```

---

## Heartbeat Execution
```csharp
private async Task<HeartbeatResponse> PerformHeartbeat()
{
    var request = new HeartbeatRequest
    {
        LicenseKey = _validator.GetStoredLicense(),
        HardwareInfo = JsonSerializer.Serialize(
            _validator.GetHardwareFingerprint()),
        ClientTimestamp = DateTime.UtcNow
    };
    
    // Sign request
    request.Signature = ComputeHMAC(request);
    
    var response = await _client.PostAsJsonAsync(
        "https://server.com/api/license/heartbeat", 
        request);
    
    var result = await response.Content
        .ReadFromJsonAsync<HeartbeatResponse>();
    
    // Verify response signature
    if (!VerifyResponseSignature(result))
        throw new SecurityException("Invalid response");
    
    return result;
}
```

---

## Local Storage
```csharp
private void SaveLastSuccessfulHeartbeat(DateTime serverTime)
{
    // Use hardware fingerprint as encryption key
    var hwKey = _validator.GetHardwareFingerprint()
        .ComputeSHA256Bytes();
    
    // Encrypt timestamp
    var encrypted = CryptoHelper.AesEncrypt(
        serverTime.ToString("O"), 
        hwKey);
    
    // Store in registry
    RegistryHelper.SetEncryptedValue("LastHeartbeat", encrypted);
}
```

::: {.notes}
Encryption with hardware key means timestamp becomes invalid if hardware changes
:::

---

## Grace Period Validation
```csharp
private bool IsWithinGracePeriod()
{
    try
    {
        // Decrypt with hardware key
        var hwKey = _validator.GetHardwareFingerprint()
            .ComputeSHA256Bytes();
        
        var encrypted = RegistryHelper
            .GetEncryptedValue("LastHeartbeat");
        
        var lastHeartbeat = DateTime.Parse(
            CryptoHelper.AesDecrypt(encrypted, hwKey));
        
        return (DateTime.UtcNow - lastHeartbeat).TotalDays 
            <= GracePeriodDays;
    }
    catch
    {
        return false; // No valid heartbeat stored
    }
}
```

---

## Network Failure Handling
```csharp
private async Task HandleNetworkFailure()
{
    // Check if still within grace period
    if (!IsWithinGracePeriod())
    {
        await HandleInvalidLicense(
            "Unable to verify license - grace period expired");
    }
    
    // Retry in 1 hour
    await Task.Delay(TimeSpan.FromHours(1));
}
```

---

# Cryptography

---

## HMAC Signatures
```csharp
public static string ComputeHMAC(string data, string secret)
{
    using var hmac = new HMACSHA256(
        Encoding.UTF8.GetBytes(secret));
    
    var hash = hmac.ComputeHash(
        Encoding.UTF8.GetBytes(data));
    
    return Convert.ToBase64String(hash);
}

public static bool VerifyHMAC(string data, string signature)
{
    var computed = ComputeHMAC(data, secret);
    
    return CryptographicOperations.FixedTimeEquals(
        Convert.FromBase64String(computed),
        Convert.FromBase64String(signature)
    );
}
```

::: {.notes}
Fixed-time comparison prevents timing attacks
:::

---

## AES Encryption
```csharp
public static string AesEncrypt(string plainText, byte[] key)
{
    using var aes = Aes.Create();
    aes.Key = DeriveKey(key, 32); // 256-bit key
    aes.GenerateIV();
    
    using var encryptor = aes.CreateEncryptor();
    using var ms = new MemoryStream();
    
    // Prepend IV to ciphertext
    ms.Write(aes.IV, 0, aes.IV.Length);
    
    using (var cs = new CryptoStream(ms, encryptor, CryptoStreamMode.Write))
    using (var sw = new StreamWriter(cs))
    {
        sw.Write(plainText);
    }
    
    return Convert.ToBase64String(ms.ToArray());
}
```

---

## AES Decryption
```csharp
public static string AesDecrypt(string cipherText, byte[] key)
{
    var fullCipher = Convert.FromBase64String(cipherText);
    
    using var aes = Aes.Create();
    aes.Key = DeriveKey(key, 32);
    
    // Extract IV from beginning
    var iv = new byte[aes.BlockSize / 8];
    var cipher = new byte[fullCipher.Length - iv.Length];
    
    Array.Copy(fullCipher, iv, iv.Length);
    Array.Copy(fullCipher, iv.Length, cipher, 0, cipher.Length);
    
    aes.IV = iv;
    
    using var decryptor = aes.CreateDecryptor();
    using var ms = new MemoryStream(cipher);
    using var cs = new CryptoStream(ms, decryptor, CryptoStreamMode.Read);
    using var sr = new StreamReader(cs);
    
    return sr.ReadToEnd();
}
```

---

## Key Derivation
```csharp
private static byte[] DeriveKey(byte[] input, int keyLength)
{
    using var sha = SHA256.Create();
    var hash = sha.ComputeHash(input);
    
    if (keyLength <= hash.Length)
        return hash.Take(keyLength).ToArray();
    
    // For longer keys, use PBKDF2
    using var pbkdf2 = new Rfc2898DeriveBytes(
        input, 
        saltBytes,
        iterations: 10000, 
        HashAlgorithmName.SHA256
    );
    
    return pbkdf2.GetBytes(keyLength);
}
```

---

# Attack Resistance

---

## Threat Model

**What we protect against:**

- License key sharing
- Offline cracking attempts
- Network interception (MITM)
- Local timestamp tampering
- Hardware changes
- Automated piracy tools

---

## Protection Mechanisms

| Attack Vector | Defense |
|--------------|---------|
| Key sharing | Hardware fingerprinting + device limits |
| Offline use | Grace period expiration |
| MITM | HMAC signature validation |
| Timestamp tampering | Hardware-bound encryption |
| Multiple machines | Server-side device tracking |
| Replay attacks | Timestamp validation |

---

## Signature Flow
```{mermaid}
sequenceDiagram
    participant C as Client
    participant S as Server
    
    C->>C: Create request data
    C->>C: Sign with HMAC(data + secret)
    C->>S: Send request + signature
    S->>S: Verify HMAC(data + secret)
    alt Valid signature
        S->>S: Process request
        S->>S: Sign response
        S->>C: Send response + signature
        C->>C: Verify response signature
    else Invalid signature
        S->>C: Unauthorized
    end
```

---

## Encryption Flow
```{mermaid}
flowchart LR
    HW[Hardware Fingerprint] -->|SHA256| Key[Encryption Key]
    TS[Server Timestamp] -->|AES Encrypt| CT[Ciphertext]
    Key --> CT
    CT -->|Store| REG[Registry]
    
    REG -->|Load| CT2[Ciphertext]
    HW2[Hardware Fingerprint] -->|SHA256| Key2[Decryption Key]
    CT2 -->|AES Decrypt| TS2[Timestamp]
    Key2 --> TS2
    
    style HW fill:#2c5282
    style HW2 fill:#2c5282
    style REG fill:#1a365d
```

---

## Tamper Detection

**Hardware change scenario:**

1. User tries to move license to new machine
2. Hardware fingerprint changes
3. Decryption key becomes invalid
4. Cannot decrypt last heartbeat timestamp
5. Grace period check fails
6. License invalidated

**Result:** License tied to original hardware

---

# Deployment

---

## Server Configuration
```csharp
// Program.cs
builder.Services.AddDbContext<LicenseDbContext>();
builder.Services.AddHttpClient();

// Store secret securely
builder.Configuration.AddAzureKeyVault(
    new Uri("https://your-vault.vault.azure.net/"));

var app = builder.Build();

app.MapPost("/api/license/heartbeat", 
    async (HeartbeatRequest req, LicenseService svc) =>
        await svc.ValidateHeartbeat(req));
```

---

## Client Registration
```csharp
// Program.cs (GProd)
builder.Services.AddHttpClient();
builder.Services.AddSingleton<ILicenseValidator, LicenseValidator>();
builder.Services.AddHostedService<LicenseHeartbeatService>();

var app = builder.Build();

// Start background service automatically
await app.RunAsync();
```

---

## Environment Variables

**Server (.env):**
```bash
LICENSE_SHARED_SECRET=your-strong-secret-min-32-chars
DATABASE_CONNECTION=Server=...;Database=Licenses;
AZURE_KEY_VAULT_URL=https://your-vault.vault.azure.net/
```

**Client:**
```bash
LICENSE_SERVER_URL=https://license.getgain.pt/api
```

---

## Database Schema
```sql
CREATE TABLE Licenses (
    Id INT PRIMARY KEY IDENTITY,
    LicenseKey NVARCHAR(100) UNIQUE NOT NULL,
    ExpiryDate DATETIME2,
    IsRevoked BIT DEFAULT 0,
    MaxDevices INT DEFAULT 1,
    CreatedAt DATETIME2 DEFAULT GETUTCDATE()
);

CREATE TABLE RegisteredDevices (
    Id INT PRIMARY KEY IDENTITY,
    LicenseId INT FOREIGN KEY REFERENCES Licenses(Id),
    FingerprintHash NVARCHAR(64) NOT NULL,
    FirstSeen DATETIME2 DEFAULT GETUTCDATE(),
    LastSeen DATETIME2
);

CREATE INDEX IX_Devices_License ON RegisteredDevices(LicenseId);
CREATE INDEX IX_Devices_Hash ON RegisteredDevices(FingerprintHash);
```

---

# Monitoring

---

## Server Metrics

Track these events:

- Failed signature validations (attack attempts)
- Device limit exceeded (sharing detection)
- Expired licenses (renewal needed)
- Revocation triggers
- Heartbeat frequency per license

---

## Logging Example
```csharp
if (!ValidateRequestSignature(request))
{
    _logger.LogWarning(
        "Invalid signature for license {License} from IP {IP}",
        request.LicenseKey.Substring(0, 8) + "...",
        httpContext.Connection.RemoteIpAddress
    );
    
    // Increment attack counter
    await _metrics.IncrementAsync("license.invalid_signature");
    
    return Unauthorized();
}
```

---

## Client Telemetry
```csharp
private async Task HandleInvalidLicense(string reason)
{
    _logger.LogError("License validation failed: {Reason}", reason);
    
    // Optional: Report to server (if network available)
    await _telemetry.ReportAsync(new {
        Event = "LicenseInvalid",
        Reason = reason,
        Timestamp = DateTime.UtcNow
    });
    
    // Disable application
    await _validator.RevokeAccess(reason);
}
```

---

# Testing

---

## Unit Tests
```csharp
[Fact]
public void HMAC_Signature_Should_Be_Verifiable()
{
    var data = "test-license-key|hardware-info|2025-01-21T10:00:00";
    var signature = CryptoHelper.ComputeHMAC(data);
    
    var isValid = CryptoHelper.VerifyHMAC(data, signature);
    
    Assert.True(isValid);
}

[Fact]
public void Tampered_Signature_Should_Fail()
{
    var data = "test-license-key|hardware-info|2025-01-21T10:00:00";
    var signature = CryptoHelper.ComputeHMAC(data);
    
    var tamperedData = data + "extra";
    var isValid = CryptoHelper.VerifyHMAC(tamperedData, signature);
    
    Assert.False(isValid);
}
```

---

## Integration Tests
```csharp
[Fact]
public async Task Valid_Heartbeat_Should_Succeed()
{
    var license = await CreateTestLicense();
    var request = new HeartbeatRequest {
        LicenseKey = license.Key,
        HardwareInfo = GetTestHardware(),
        ClientTimestamp = DateTime.UtcNow
    };
    request.Signature = SignRequest(request);
    
    var response = await _client.PostAsJsonAsync(
        "/api/license/heartbeat", request);
    
    Assert.Equal(HttpStatusCode.OK, response.StatusCode);
    var result = await response.Content
        .ReadFromJsonAsync<HeartbeatResponse>();
    Assert.True(result.Valid);
}
```

---

## Grace Period Test
```csharp
[Fact]
public void Expired_Grace_Period_Should_Fail()
{
    // Save old heartbeat (8 days ago)
    var oldTimestamp = DateTime.UtcNow.AddDays(-8);
    var hwKey = GetHardwareFingerprint().ComputeSHA256Bytes();
    var encrypted = CryptoHelper.AesEncrypt(
        oldTimestamp.ToString("O"), hwKey);
    RegistryHelper.SetEncryptedValue("LastHeartbeat", encrypted);
    
    // Check grace period
    var service = new LicenseHeartbeatService(_validator, _factory);
    var withinGrace = service.IsWithinGracePeriod();
    
    Assert.False(withinGrace);
}
```

---

# Best Practices

---

## Security Checklist

- [ ] Use HTTPS only (enforce TLS 1.2+)
- [ ] Store secrets in Azure Key Vault
- [ ] Implement rate limiting (100 req/hour per IP)
- [ ] Log all validation failures
- [ ] Rotate shared secret periodically
- [ ] Use certificate pinning in production
- [ ] Validate timestamp drift (±5 minutes)
- [ ] Monitor for brute force attempts

---

## Operational Guidelines

**Grace period:**

- 7 days for typical usage
- Longer for industries with poor connectivity
- Shorter for high-security requirements

**Heartbeat interval:**

- 24 hours ± 2 hours (jitter)
- Too frequent = server load
- Too infrequent = delayed revocation

**Device limits:**

- 1-3 for individual licenses
- 5-10 for team licenses
- Unlimited for enterprise

---

## User Experience

**Handle failures gracefully:**
```csharp
private async Task HandleInvalidLicense(string reason)
{
    var message = reason switch
    {
        "License expired" => 
            "Your license has expired. Please renew at...",
        "Device limit exceeded" => 
            "This license is already in use on the maximum number of devices.",
        "License revoked" => 
            "This license has been revoked. Contact support at...",
        _ => 
            "Unable to validate license. Please check your connection."
    };
    
    await ShowUserDialog(message);
}
```

---

## Maintenance

**Regular tasks:**

- Review device registrations (detect anomalies)
- Clean up expired licenses (database cleanup)
- Monitor heartbeat success rates
- Update hardware fingerprint logic (OS updates)
- Test grace period logic (simulate network failures)

---

# Summary

---

## System Benefits

✅ **Strong protection** - Multiple security layers

✅ **Hardware binding** - Device-specific licenses

✅ **Remote control** - Server-side revocation

✅ **Offline grace** - 7-day buffer for network issues

✅ **Tamper resistant** - Cryptographic validation

✅ **Scalable** - Supports thousands of clients

---

## Key Takeaways

1. **Defense in depth** - Combine multiple techniques
2. **User-friendly** - Grace period prevents false positives
3. **Cryptographically secure** - HMAC + AES-256
4. **Observable** - Logging and metrics built-in
5. **Maintainable** - Clean separation of concerns

---

## Next Steps

**Implementation:**

1. Deploy license server (Azure/AWS)
2. Integrate client service into GProd
3. Test thoroughly (unit + integration)
4. Configure monitoring and alerts
5. Document customer onboarding

**Resources:**

- Source code: [GitHub repository]
- Documentation: [Wiki/Confluence]
- Support: licensing@getgain.pt

---

## Questions?

**Contact:**

Gustavo Reis  
Senior AI Engineer & R&D Director  
GETGAIN Solutions  

Email: gustavo@getgain.pt

---

## Appendix: Hardware Fingerprint
```csharp
public class HardwareFingerprint
{
    public string CpuId { get; set; }
    public string MacAddress { get; set; }
    public string DiskSerial { get; set; }
    public string MotherboardSerial { get; set; }
    
    public static HardwareFingerprint Generate()
    {
        return new HardwareFingerprint
        {
            CpuId = GetCpuId(),
            MacAddress = GetMacAddress(),
            DiskSerial = GetDiskSerial(),
            MotherboardSerial = GetMotherboardSerial()
        };
    }
    
    private static string GetCpuId()
    {
        using var searcher = new ManagementObjectSearcher(
            "SELECT ProcessorId FROM Win32_Processor");
        return searcher.Get().Cast<ManagementObject>()
            .First()["ProcessorId"].ToString();
    }
    
    // Similar methods for MAC, disk, motherboard...
}
```

---

## Appendix: Rate Limiting
```csharp
public class RateLimitMiddleware
{
    private static readonly ConcurrentDictionary<string, RateLimitInfo> 
        _requests = new();
    
    public async Task InvokeAsync(HttpContext context, RequestDelegate next)
    {
        var ip = context.Connection.RemoteIpAddress?.ToString();
        var info = _requests.GetOrAdd(ip, _ => new RateLimitInfo());
        
        if (!info.AllowRequest())
        {
            context.Response.StatusCode = 429; // Too Many Requests
            await context.Response.WriteAsync(
                "Rate limit exceeded. Try again later.");
            return;
        }
        
        await next(context);
    }
}

public class RateLimitInfo
{
    private Queue<DateTime> _requests = new();
    private const int MaxRequests = 100;
    private static readonly TimeSpan Window = TimeSpan.FromHours(1);
    
    public bool AllowRequest()
    {
        var now = DateTime.UtcNow;
        _requests = new Queue<DateTime>(
            _requests.Where(t => now - t < Window));
        
        if (_requests.Count >= MaxRequests)
            return false;
        
        _requests.Enqueue(now);
        return true;
    }
}
```
